602:def _relevance_drop_ledger_enabled() -> bool:
603:    """Kill-switch `PG_RELEVANCE_DROP_LEDGER` (default ON under semantic_v2). When
604:    ON, every below-floor relevance drop is logged with its cosine score + url so
605:    the operator can audit the filter. Telemetry-only — never changes the keep
650:    )
651:
652:
653:def _semantic_relevance_scores(
654:    research_question: str,
655:    sub_queries: list[str] | None,
656:    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
657:) -> dict[int, float] | None:
658:    """Batch embedding-cosine relevance for every row.
659:
660:    Returns ``{row_index -> max cosine over {research_question} ∪ {sub_queries}}``
661:    clamped to [0, 1], or ``None`` if the embedder is unavailable / scoring fails
662:    (the caller then falls back LOUDLY to the lexical scorer — never a silent
663:    keep-all, LAW II).
664:
665:    The MAX over the question AND each sub-query is the cosine-space analogue of
666:    `_row_relevance_facet`'s max-over-subqueries: a row matching ONE focused facet
667:    clears the floor instead of being diluted. Because the score is cosine (not a
668:    fraction over the whole-question token set), the long-question denominator that
669:    buried on-topic papers is gone at the root — but we still take the per-subquery
670:    max so a multi-facet question routes each row to its best-matching facet. All
671:    anchors are embedded in the SAME cosine space (no scale-mixing — the silent bug
672:    `_row_relevance_facet` would have if base became cosine while subquery stayed
673:    lexical).
674:    """
675:    embedder = _get_semantic_embedder()
676:    if embedder is None:
677:        return None
678:    anchors: list[str] = []
679:    q = (research_question or "").strip()
680:    if q:
681:        anchors.append(q)
682:    for sq in sub_queries or []:
683:        s = str(sq or "").strip()
684:        if s:
685:            anchors.append(s)
686:    if not anchors:
687:        # No usable anchor text — cannot score semantically; fall back loudly.
688:        _LOGGER.warning(
689:            "[select] semantic relevance: empty research_question AND no "
690:            "sub-queries — falling back to the lexical scorer."
691:        )
692:        return None
693:    row_texts = [_row_embed_text(row) for row in evidence_rows]
694:    try:
695:        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.prefetch_offtopic_filter import (
696:            _similarity_scores,
697:        )
698:        # One embed pass per anchor over ALL row texts (batched inside
699:        # `_similarity_scores`); take the per-row MAX across anchors. Rows are
700:        # embedded once per anchor (anchors are few: 1 question + a handful of
701:        # sub-queries), NEVER once per row.
702:        per_anchor: list[list[float]] = [
703:            _similarity_scores(embedder, anchor, row_texts) for anchor in anchors
704:        ]
705:    except Exception as exc:
706:        _LOGGER.warning(
707:            "[select] semantic relevance scoring failed (%s) — falling back to "
708:            "the lexical scorer.",
709:            str(exc)[:200],
710:        )
711:        return None
712:    scores: dict[int, float] = {}
713:    for i in range(len(evidence_rows)):
714:        best = 0.0
715:        for sims in per_anchor:
716:            if i < len(sims):
717:                v = sims[i]
718:                if v > best:
719:                    best = v
720:        # Clamp to [0, 1]: cosine can be negative (off-topic); a negative score is
721:        # floored to 0.0 so it reads as "no relevance" and a row with no embeddable
722:        # text (empty statement+quote) scores 0.0 rather than being laundered up.
723:        scores[i] = min(1.0, max(0.0, float(best)))
724:    return scores
725:
1905:        return False

1907:    # I-arch-002 (#1246) P-W1 — WEIGHT, don't FILTER (DNA §-1.3). Under the master
1908:    # redesign flag the relevance "floor" stopped HARD-DROPPING below-floor rows:
1909:    # every scored row was KEPT carrying its LEXICAL relevance score. That keep-all
1910:    # was right for CREDIBILITY (a low-tier source may carry a real finding) but
1911:    # WRONG for topical RELEVANCE — it let off-topic noise into the pot because the
1912:    # lexical scorer was the only filter and it was broken (the long-question
1913:    # denominator buried on-topic T1 papers, so keep-all was the lesser evil).
1914:    #
1915:    # B1 (b1b10 redesign): with a SEMANTIC scorer (`semantic_mode`), relevance is a
1916:    # trustworthy filter again, so RESTORE it — keep rows whose cosine relevance
1917:    # >= floor OR floor-exempt. This is the relevance/credibility axis split: the
1918:    # SEMANTIC score FILTERS here; credibility/authority/retrieval_weight still only
1919:    # WEIGHT the sort below. Off-topic is genuinely useless at any weight (the one
1920:    # axis §-1.3 "weight don't filter" does not govern). The faithfulness engine is
1921:    # untouched.
1922:    #
1923:    # B1 fallback (Codex diff-gate P0): whenever the operator REQUESTED the semantic
1924:    # scorer (`semantic_requested`), the filter MUST be restored — even when the
1925:    # embedder was unavailable and we degraded to the lexical score
1926:    # (`semantic_requested and not semantic_mode`). Otherwise, under
1927:    # PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN, an embedder-unavailable run would silently fall
1928:    # into the keep-all branch (`_redesign_on and ...`), violating the hard
1929:    # constraint "embedder-unavailable must LOUDLY fall back to the lexical FILTER,
1930:    # never a silent keep-all". So keep-all is preserved ONLY when semantic was NOT
1931:    # requested at all (the true legacy path) => byte-identical default.
1932:    _redesign_on = _credibility_redesign_enabled()
1933:    _restore_filter = semantic_mode or semantic_requested
1934:    if _redesign_on and not _restore_filter:
1935:        kept = list(scored)
2140:    _semantic_scores: dict[int, float] | None = None
2141:    _semantic_fell_back = False
2142:    if relevance_floor is not None and _semantic_scorer_enabled():
2143:        _semantic_scores = _semantic_relevance_scores(
2144:            research_question, sub_queries, evidence_rows,
2145:        )
2146:        if _semantic_scores is None:
2147:            _semantic_fell_back = True
2148:            _LOGGER.warning(
2149:                "[select] PG_RELEVANCE_SCORER=semantic_v2 requested but the "

2153:            )

2164:            )
2165:        scored.append((idx, score, tier, row))

3138:# scorer (one place, one story) if it is wanted at all — it is NOT re-added here.
3139:#
3140:# CREDIBILITY/TIER IS NEVER A DROP HERE — only TOPICAL relevance gates (off-topic
3141:# is useless at any weight). The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / 4-role D8 /
3142:# provenance) lives in the generator/evaluator and is UNTOUCHED: this lane only
3143:# changes the pre-fetch candidate menu + adds an additive weight + telemetry.
3144:#
3145:# GATING: `PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE` (default OFF). OFF => the legacy
3146:# `_rerank_and_reserve` count-cut runs byte-identically (the embedder is never
3147:# even imported — preserving `test_no_embedder_model_loaded`). ON + embedder
3148:# unavailable => LOUD fallback to the legacy lexical path (LAW II: no silent
3149:# degrade, never a silent keep-all).
3150:
3151:# B4 relevance floor — ONE relevance story across B1+B4 (Codex iter-1 P1.1).
3152:# The threshold is NOT a B4-private constant; it is B1's `PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`
3153:# (default 0.30, `evidence_selector._DEFAULT_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`), parsed by B1's
3154:# `evidence_selector.parse_relevance_floor`. B4 and B1 therefore gate on the
3155:# IDENTICAL floor against the IDENTICAL [0,1]-clamped semantic cosine — a candidate
3156:# B1's selector would keep (`item[1] >= relevance_floor` at evidence_selector.py
3157:# _relevance_floor_selection) is never pre-fetch-dropped here by a tighter B4-only
3158:# number. A cosine below this floor against EVERY anchor (question + each sub-query)
3159:# = off-topic. The floor is consumed only on the B4 ON path
3160:# (PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1); OFF => the legacy count-cut runs byte-identically
3161:# and the floor is never even read. `parse_relevance_floor` is imported lazily
3162:# inside `_relevance_gate_threshold` so the OFF path never imports evidence_selector.
3163:
3164:def _relevance_gate_enabled() -> bool:
3165:    """Kill-switch `PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE` (default OFF). ON only on an
3166:    explicit truthy ('1'/'true'/'yes'/'on'). OFF (incl. unset / any other value)
3167:    => the legacy `_rerank_and_reserve` count-cut runs byte-identically AND the
3168:    semantic embedder is never imported."""
3169:    raw = os.environ.get("PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE", "0").strip().lower()
3170:    return raw in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")

3172:
3238:def _candidate_relevance_scores(
3239:    research_question: str,
3240:    sub_queries: list[str],
3241:    candidates: list["SearchCandidate"],
3242:) -> Optional[list[float]]:
3243:    """Per-candidate topical relevance, computed by REUSING B1's scorer wholesale
3244:    (`evidence_selector._semantic_relevance_scores`) — NO B4-private scoring loop,
3245:    NO B4-private canary, so the scorer contract cannot drift (Codex B4 iter-2 P1).
3246:
3247:    Each candidate is shaped into the row-dict B1's `_row_embed_text` reads. That
3248:    helper embeds the row's ``statement`` + ``direct_quote`` fields, so a
3249:    candidate's text surface (`snippet_text` = title + snippet — the SAME surface
3250:    the lexical scorer reads) is mapped onto ``statement``. B1 then returns
3251:    ``{row_index -> max cosine over {research_question} ∪ {sub_queries}}`` clamped
3252:    to [0, 1], or ``None`` (embedder unavailable / scoring failed / no anchor).
3253:
3254:    Returns a per-candidate score list aligned to ``candidates`` (in [0, 1]), or
3255:    ``None`` — the caller falls back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical cut on ``None``
3256:    (LAW II: no silent keep-all). The import is INSIDE this function so the OFF path
3257:    (which never calls it) never imports the embedder / evidence_selector."""
3258:    try:
3259:        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.evidence_selector import (
3260:            _semantic_relevance_scores,
3261:        )
3262:    except Exception as exc:  # import-path failure
3263:        logger.warning(
3264:            "[live_retriever] B4 relevance scorer import failed (%s) — falling "
3265:            "back LOUDLY to the lexical count-cut.",
3266:            str(exc)[:200],
3267:        )
3268:        return None
3269:    # Shape each candidate as the row-dict B1's `_row_embed_text` reads: it embeds
3270:    # `statement` + `direct_quote`, so the candidate's title+snippet surface goes
3271:    # onto `statement` (an empty-snippet candidate therefore embeds to ~0.0, exactly
3272:    # as B1 scores a row with no text — no B4-private bypass).
3273:    rows = [
3274:        {"statement": (getattr(c, "snippet_text", "") or "")}
3275:        for c in candidates
3276:    ]
3277:    score_map = _semantic_relevance_scores(research_question, sub_queries, rows)
3278:    if score_map is None:
3279:        # B1's None = embedder unavailable / scoring failed / no usable anchor.
3280:        return None
3281:    # `_semantic_relevance_scores` keys by row index; project back to list order.
3282:    return [float(score_map.get(i, 0.0)) for i in range(len(candidates))]
3283:

3285:def _relevance_threshold_select(
3286:    candidates: list["SearchCandidate"],
3287:    *,
3288:    research_question: str,
3289:    sub_queries: list[str],
3290:    fetch_cap: int,
3291:    n_seed_injected: int,
3292:) -> tuple[list["SearchCandidate"], dict[str, float], Optional[RelevanceGateResult]]:
3293:    """B4 ON-path selection: relevance THRESHOLD (topical gate) + fetch BUDGET
3294:    (cost cap), using B1's semantic embedding-cosine scorer.
3295:
3296:    Pipeline:
3297:      1. Split seeds (`source in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS`) — NEVER scored, NEVER
3298:         dropped, prepended exactly as `_rerank_and_reserve` does (seed lane
3299:         preserved: primary-trial DOI seeds carry empty title/snippet).
3300:      2. Score every non-seed by REUSING B1's `_semantic_relevance_scores` (max
3301:         cosine over {question} ∪ {sub-queries}) — no B4-private scoring loop.
3302:      3. THRESHOLD: drop BELOW-threshold (off-topic) candidates with B1's IDENTICAL
3303:         `score >= floor` predicate (no empty-snippet bypass) — topical relevance is
3304:         the only axis allowed to gate (§-1.3). Credibility/tier is NEVER consulted.
3305:      4. BUDGET: keep the on-topic survivors in DESCENDING relevance up to
3306:         `fetch_cap` (a pure COST budget). The above-threshold-but-beyond-budget
3307:         tail is RECORDED in `RelevanceGateResult.unfetched_relevant_tail` (count
3308:         + score band), not silently discarded.
3309:      5. Carry each kept candidate's relevance score forward as a WEIGHT
3310:         (`relevance_weight` on the returned score map, keyed by url).
3311:
3312:    Returns `(selected_candidates, url_to_relevance_weight, gate_telemetry)`.
3313:    Selected order: seeds first (arrival order), then on-topic survivors in
3314:    ARRIVAL order among the budget-selected set (a stable corpus, mirroring
3315:    `_rerank_and_reserve`'s stable-order emit). On any scorer failure returns
3316:    `(None, {}, None)` so the caller falls back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical cut.
3317:
3318:    `n_seed_injected` is accepted for SIGNATURE PARITY with `_rerank_and_reserve`
3319:    (the caller passes the same args to either path); seeds are handled here by the
3320:    `source in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS` split, so the count itself is not read.
3321:    """
3322:    seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
3323:    non_seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") not in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
3324:    threshold = _relevance_gate_threshold()
3325:
3326:    if not non_seeds:
3327:        # Only seeds present — nothing to score; budget bounds the (empty) non-seeds.
3328:        gate = RelevanceGateResult(threshold=threshold, scorer="semantic_v2")
3329:        return seeds + non_seeds[:max(fetch_cap, 0)], {}, gate
3330:
3331:    scores = _candidate_relevance_scores(research_question, sub_queries, non_seeds)
3332:    if scores is None:
3333:        # LOUD fallback: signal the caller to run the legacy lexical count-cut.
3334:        return None, {}, None  # type: ignore[return-value]
3335:
3336:    budget = max(0, int(fetch_cap))
3337:    # (score, arrival_index, candidate) for stable, deterministic ordering.
3338:    scored = list(zip(scores, range(len(non_seeds)), non_seeds))
3339:    # IDENTICAL B1 keep predicate (Codex B4 iter-2 P1): `score >= floor`, exactly the
3340:    # `item[1] >= relevance_floor` B1 applies at `_relevance_floor_selection`. NO
3341:    # B4-private empty-snippet bypass: a candidate with no embeddable text scores 0.0
3342:    # under B1's scorer and therefore drops below the floor — the SAME way B1 treats a
3343:    # row with no text. Seeds (the floor-EXEMPT lane, B1's primary-trial-anchor
3344:    # analogue) are already split out above and never scored, so they are never
3345:    # dropped here; the only thing this floor drops is a genuinely text-less non-seed,
3346:    # which is the documented reconciliation (do not diverge from B1's predicate).
3347:    on_topic = [t for t in scored if t[0] >= threshold]
3348:    off_topic = [t for t in scored if t[0] < threshold]
3349:
3350:    # BUDGET: rank on-topic survivors by (-score, arrival_index); the top `budget`
3351:    # are fetched, the rest are the unfetched-but-relevant tail (a COST drop).
3352:    on_topic_ranked = sorted(on_topic, key=lambda t: (-t[0], t[1]))
3353:    fetched = on_topic_ranked[:budget]
3354:    tail = on_topic_ranked[budget:]
3355:
3356:    selected_idx = {t[1] for t in fetched}
3357:    # Stable corpus: emit fetched non-seeds in ARRIVAL order among the selected set.
3358:    selected_non_seeds = [c for i, c in enumerate(non_seeds) if i in selected_idx]
3359:
3360:    # Carry the relevance score forward as a weight, keyed by url (kept set only).
3361:    url_weight: dict[str, float] = {}
3362:    for score, idx, cand in fetched:
3363:        url_weight[cand.url] = float(score)
3364:
3365:    tail_scores = [t[0] for t in tail]
3366:    gate = RelevanceGateResult(
3367:        threshold=threshold,
3368:        total_scored=len(non_seeds),
3369:        kept_on_topic=len(on_topic),
3370:        dropped_off_topic=len(off_topic),
3371:        fetched_budget=len(fetched),
3372:        unfetched_relevant_tail=len(tail),
3373:        tail_score_min=(min(tail_scores) if tail_scores else None),
3374:        tail_score_max=(max(tail_scores) if tail_scores else None),
3375:        scorer="semantic_v2",
3376:    )
3377:    return seeds + selected_non_seeds, url_weight, gate
3770:    _b4_relevance_weights: dict[str, float] = {}

1:"""B9 domain-pack loader.
2:
3:Loads the per-domain configuration packs in ``config/domain_packs/`` — each
4:pack owns the domain-specific WEIGHTS and LABELS (report sections, contradiction
5:predicates, qualitative lexicon pointer, source-tier credibility priors, safety
6:policy). A pack is NEVER a hard DROP/CAP; the faithfulness engine remains the
7:only hard gate (CLAUDE.md §-1.3).
8:
9:POLARIS is GENERAL by default: an unknown / blank / unrecognised domain resolves
10:to the ``general`` pack — NEVER ``clinical``. The clinical pack is the only one
11:with ``is_clinical: true``; it is selected only when the clinical domain is
12:positively detected (``domain_signal.is_clinical_domain``).
13:
14:LAW VI: no hard-coded pack content — everything is read from YAML. Fail-loud on a
15:malformed pack (a required key missing), but NEVER fail on an unknown domain
16:(degrade to general).
111:def load_domain_pack(domain: Optional[str]) -> dict[str, Any]:
112:    """Load the pack for ``domain``; degrade to the ``general`` pack on unknown.
113:
114:    Never raises on an unknown domain (operator-locked: unknown -> general,
115:    never clinical, never abort). Raises only when the RESOLVED pack file is
116:    malformed (fail-loud) or when neither the requested pack NOR the general
117:    fallback exists on disk (a real config-integrity error).
118:    """
119:    name = normalize_domain(domain)
120:    # Resolve a canonical-8 scope token onto its descriptively-named pack file.
121:    name = _DOMAIN_PACK_ALIASES.get(name, name)
122:    if name in _pack_cache:
123:        return _pack_cache[name]
124:    d = _packs_dir()
125:    path = d / f"{name}.yaml"
126:    if not path.exists():
127:        # Unknown domain -> general fallback (never clinical).
128:        if name != GENERAL_DOMAIN:
129:            return load_domain_pack(GENERAL_DOMAIN)
130:        raise RuntimeError(
131:            f"general domain pack not found at {path}. config/domain_packs/"
132:            f"general.yaml is required (the domain-agnostic default)."
133:        )
134:    raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or {}
135:    pack = _validate_pack(name, raw)
136:    _pack_cache[name] = pack
137:    return pack

1:"""B9 domain-generalization spine — the deterministic ``is_clinical`` backbone.
2:
3:POLARIS is GENERAL by default; clinical rigor is a DETECTED specialization
4:layered on top (dual-agreed B1-B10 plan, B9 "the spine"). The historical
5:failure (the 2,738 junk clinical-contradiction flags on a labor-displacement
6:report) was NOT "a domain param wasn't read" alone — it was that the clinical
7:predicate/lexicon logic fired UNCONDITIONALLY on every domain. The fix is a
8:single, deterministic, NO-LLM ``is_clinical`` signal threaded into every
9:consumer (qualitative_conflict_detector, contradiction_detector, finding_dedup,
10:multi_section_generator). When ``is_clinical`` is False the clinical-only
11:predicate unions / NegEx lexicon / clinical few-shots do not apply; the
12:domain-agnostic path runs instead. The faithfulness engine (strict_verify /
13:span-grounding / NLI / 4-role) is unchanged and remains the only hard gate.
14:
15:Design rules (operator-locked B9 constraints):
55:def normalize_domain(domain: Optional[str]) -> str:
56:    """Lowercase + trim a domain string; blank/None -> ``GENERAL_DOMAIN``.
57:
58:    Pure. Never raises. The single place "" -> general is enforced so no
59:    consumer accidentally falls back to clinical.
60:    """
61:    d = (domain or "").strip().lower()
62:    return d if d else GENERAL_DOMAIN
145:def is_clinical_domain(
146:    domain: Optional[str],
147:    evidence: Optional[Iterable[Any]] = None,
148:) -> bool:
149:    """Return True iff the run should take the CLINICAL specialization.
150:
151:    Deterministic, no LLM, never raises. The single ``is_clinical`` signal
152:    every B9 consumer reads.
153:
154:    Routing (in order):
155:      * ``domain == "clinical"`` (case/space-insensitive) -> True. This is the
156:        explicit positive route the sweep takes for clinical questions, so the
157:        clinical path is byte-identical to today.
158:      * Any OTHER known/non-blank domain (workforce/policy/tech/economics/
159:        general/custom/...) -> False. A non-clinical domain NEVER routes
160:        clinical even if the text mentions a clinical-sounding word.
161:      * Blank / None domain -> probe the evidence text for a positive clinical
162:        intervention or population signal; True only on a positive signal,
163:        else False. This keeps a genuinely-clinical corpus with no domain hint
164:        (tests / legacy callers that pass ``domain=None``) on the clinical
165:        path with no regression, while a blank non-clinical corpus stays
166:        general.
167:    """
168:    d = (domain or "").strip().lower()
169:    if d == CLINICAL_DOMAIN:
170:        return True
171:    if d:
172:        # A positively-named non-clinical domain is authoritative.
173:        return False
174:    # Blank/unknown domain: degrade to a positive text signal, never to
175:    # clinical-by-default.
176:    return _has_clinical_text_signal(evidence)

77:# B9 domain-generalization — DOMAIN-AGNOSTIC numeric extraction.
78:#
79:# The clinical extractor (`extract_numeric_claims` clinical path) keys a claim
80:# on a CLINICAL predicate lexicon (`_EFFICACY_PREDICATES` / `_SAFETY_PREDICATES`)
81:# and a CLINICAL subject (`_DRUG_NAME_RE`). For a non-clinical corpus
82:# (economics / AI-labor / policy / science / tech) both return nothing, so
83:# `extract_numeric_claims` emits ZERO claims → every non-clinical numeric row
84:# becomes a SINGLETON in `finding_dedup` / `claim_graph` (the documented
85:# residual that blocks B6/B8 non-clinical baskets). The generic path below
86:# extracts a claim-atom WITHOUT any clinical literal: a generic metric cue (the
87:# SAME field-agnostic cues as `scope_gate.extract_research_frame_heuristic`) is
88:# the predicate, a generic number+unit is the value, and a generic content-word
89:# entity is the subject. This yields a REAL claim-key for non-clinical numerics
90:# (so corroborating sources can consolidate into a basket) WITHOUT loosening the
91:# clinical merge rule — clinical runs are byte-identical (gated on is_clinical).
92:# Faithfulness is unchanged: span-grounding / strict_verify remain the hard gate.
281:def _extract_numeric_claims_generic(
282:    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
283:) -> list["ExtractedNumericClaim"]:
284:    """DOMAIN-AGNOSTIC numeric claim extraction for NON-clinical corpora.
285:
286:    One claim per row at most (the first generic metric value), keyed on a
287:    generic predicate + generic subject + value + unit. A row with no generic
288:    metric cue OR no extractable number yields NO claim (kept as a safe
289:    singleton upstream). The clinical discriminator fields (dose / arm / Wave-3)
290:    stay at their UNKNOWN defaults so they never anchor a non-clinical merge —
291:    a non-clinical merge is anchored ONLY by a confidently-extracted
292:    subject+predicate+value+unit (the conservative rule). NO clinical literal.
293:    """
294:    claims: list[ExtractedNumericClaim] = []
295:    for ev in evidence:
296:        quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
297:        if not quote:
298:            continue
299:        predicate = _normalize_predicate_generic(quote)
300:        if not predicate:
301:            continue
302:        # Anchor the value on the number nearest the metric cue (Codex P1.4) so
303:        # a leading date / sample-size cannot become the claim value/key.
304:        _cue_m = re.search(re.escape(predicate), quote, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
305:        _cue_pos = _cue_m.start() if _cue_m else None
306:        found = _find_value_generic(quote, cue_pos=_cue_pos)
307:        if found is None:
308:            continue
309:        value, unit, ctx_window, anchor_pos = found
310:        subject = _subject_generic(quote, anchor_pos, predicate=predicate)
311:        # Codex iter-3 P1: do NOT set endpoint_phrase=predicate. The metric cue
312:        # IS the predicate, not a time-window/endpoint. Stamping it as
313:        # endpoint_phrase would make _shared_metric_axes read two same-predicate
314:        # generic claims as POSITIVELY-confirmed shared scope -> a hard
315:        # contradiction, when in fact their comparator/population/time-window are
316:        # UNCONFIRMED. Leaving the discriminator axes empty means an unconfirmed
317:        # generic numeric gap is correctly labeled possible_metric_mismatch
318:        # (conservative non-clinical default); a true contradiction needs a
319:        # positively-extracted shared scope axis. The predicate itself still
320:        # groups the claims (grouping key includes predicate).
321:        claims.append(ExtractedNumericClaim(
322:            evidence_id=str(ev.get("evidence_id", "")),
323:            subject=subject,
324:            predicate=predicate,
325:            value=value,
326:            unit=unit,
327:            context_snippet=ctx_window[:200],
328:            source_url=ev.get("source_url", ""),
329:            source_tier=ev.get("tier", ""),
330:        ))
331:    return claims

1062:    domain: str | None = None,
1063:) -> list[ExtractedNumericClaim]:
1064:    """Extract structured numeric claims from evidence rows.
1065:
1066:    Each evidence dict should have 'evidence_id', 'direct_quote' (or
1067:    'statement'), 'source_url', 'tier'. Missing fields are handled.
1068:
1069:    BUG-M-202 fix (deep-dive R7): `domain` parameter routes to a
1070:    broader predicate set for non-clinical queries. Default None
1071:    preserves the union-fallback (original behavior).
1072:
1073:    B9 domain-generalization: for a NON-clinical run (deterministic
1074:    `is_clinical_domain` over `domain` + the evidence text) the clinical
1075:    predicate union + `_DRUG_NAME_RE` subject extract NOTHING, so this
1076:    historically returned zero claims and every non-clinical numeric became a
1077:    singleton. We now route non-clinical corpora to the DOMAIN-AGNOSTIC
1078:    extractor (generic metric cue + generic subject + value + unit), which
1079:    yields a real claim-key so corroborating sources can consolidate. The
1080:    CLINICAL path (is_clinical True — `domain="clinical"`, or a blank domain
1081:    over a clinically-signalled corpus) is byte-identical to before.
1082:    """
1083:    from src.polaris_graph.domain.domain_signal import is_clinical_domain
1084:    if not is_clinical_domain(domain, evidence):
1085:        return _extract_numeric_claims_generic(evidence)

641:# B9 domain-generalization (the spine) — cheap domain/intent classifier.
642:#
643:# SG1 fix: POLARIS historically DEFAULTED to clinical and never INFERRED the
644:# domain, so a non-clinical question silently ran the clinical path. This adds a
645:# lightweight, INJECTABLE domain/intent classifier that returns a free-text
646:# domain (NOT a closed enum — avoids re-introducing the closed-set trap one
647:# level up), the deterministic capability flags `is_clinical` / `is_quantitative`,
648:# and `key_entity_types`. It DEGRADES gracefully: a wrong/blank classification
649:# falls to `general` (weight, never abort, never clinical). The output is
650:# ADVISORY routing context — it is NOT serialized into the immutable
651:# `protocol.json`, so adding it does NOT change the protocol bytes/hash (clinical
652:# byte-identity preserved).
653:#
654:# The LLM seam is OPTIONAL + injectable. The DEFAULT is a deterministic, NO-SPEND
655:# heuristic (reuses the `is_clinical_domain` backbone + the field-agnostic frame
656:# heuristic), so build/smoke never hit a live LLM. A caller may inject an LLM
657:# callable (`Callable[[str], str]` returning JSON) to refine the free-text domain
658:# label; an LLM error/garbage output FAILS OPEN to the heuristic (general).
709:def classify_domain_intent(
710:    question: str,
711:    *,
712:    evidence: Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]] = None,
713:    domain_hint: Optional[str] = None,
714:    llm: Optional[Any] = None,
715:) -> DomainIntent:
716:    """Classify the question's domain + intent (B9 SG1). Deterministic by
717:    default; an optional injected `llm` callable (``Callable[[str], str]``
718:    returning a JSON object with a ``domain`` key) only REFINES the free-text
719:    label. Never raises, never aborts, FAILS OPEN to `general`.
720:
721:    Args:
722:        question: the raw research question.
723:        evidence: optional evidence rows; used by the deterministic clinical
724:            signal when `domain_hint` is blank.
725:        domain_hint: optional caller-supplied domain (e.g. the sweep's
726:            `q["domain"]`). An explicit non-blank hint is authoritative for the
727:            clinical signal (so `domain="clinical"` -> is_clinical True).
728:        llm: optional injectable seam (``Callable[[str], str]``). Tests inject a
729:            deterministic fake; production may inject a real Writer call. An LLM
730:            error or unparseable output is swallowed -> heuristic label.
731:
732:    Returns:
733:        DomainIntent. `is_clinical` is the deterministic backbone signal;
734:        `domain` is a free-text label degrading to `general`.
735:    """
736:    from src.polaris_graph.domain.domain_signal import (
737:        GENERAL_DOMAIN as _GENERAL,
738:        is_clinical_domain,
739:        normalize_domain,
740:    )
741:    is_clinical = is_clinical_domain(domain_hint, evidence)
742:    is_quant = _is_quantitative_question(question)
743:    frame = extract_research_frame_heuristic(question)
744:    key_entity_types = frame.get("entities", [])[:6]
745:
746:    # Default deterministic label.
747:    label = _heuristic_domain_label(question, is_clinical)
748:    source = "heuristic"
749:
750:    # Optional LLM refinement (fail-open). The LLM may only OVERRIDE the
751:    # free-text label for a non-clinical question; it NEVER overrides the
752:    # deterministic clinical signal (clinical safety stays deterministic).
753:    if llm is not None and not is_clinical:
754:        try:
755:            raw = llm(question)
756:            parsed = json.loads(raw) if isinstance(raw, str) else (raw or {})
757:            llm_domain = normalize_domain(str(parsed.get("domain", "")))
758:            # Codex P2.2: the LLM may refine a NON-clinical free-text label but
759:            # must NEVER set the label to "clinical" when the deterministic
760:            # is_clinical signal is False — clinical routing stays deterministic
761:            # (a clinical label with is_clinical False would be inconsistent and
762:            # could reactivate clinical routing in a downstream consumer).
763:            if (
764:                llm_domain
765:                and llm_domain != _GENERAL
766:                and llm_domain != "clinical"
767:            ):
768:                label = llm_domain
769:                source = "llm"
770:        except Exception:
771:            # Garbage / error -> keep the deterministic heuristic label.
772:            label = _heuristic_domain_label(question, is_clinical)
773:            source = "heuristic"
774:
775:    return DomainIntent(
776:        domain=label or _GENERAL,
777:        is_clinical=is_clinical,
778:        is_quantitative=is_quant,
779:        key_entity_types=list(key_entity_types),
780:        source=source,
781:    )
827:    # B9 (Codex P1.1): a BLANK / whitespace-only domain is NOT an "unsupported
828:    # domain" error — it is the domain-LESS case that must route to the
829:    # domain-agnostic general default (operator-locked: "" -> general, never
830:    # abort). Normalize it to DEFAULT_DOMAIN (general) BEFORE the unsupported
831:    # check so it proceeds. A genuinely malformed explicit literal (e.g.
832:    # "made_up_domain") still falls through to the fail-loud reject below.
833:    if not (domain or "").strip():
834:        domain = DEFAULT_DOMAIN
835:
836:    # Hard reject #1: unsupported domain. A blank domain was normalized to
837:    # general above; only a non-blank UNRECOGNISED literal reaches here and
838:    # rejects loudly (fail-loud, never a silent clinical fallback).
839:    if domain not in SUPPORTED_DOMAINS:
840:        scope_decision = "reject"
841:        scope_rejected = True
842:        scope_rejection_code = "unsupported_domain"
843:        scope_reasons.append(
844:            f"domain={domain!r} is not in SUPPORTED_DOMAINS. "
845:            f"Options: {sorted(SUPPORTED_DOMAINS)}."
846:        )

1869:        # The resolver helpers are imported at module top so an IMPORT failure is loud

215:def scan_gemma_defaults() -> list[str]:
216:    """Scan live src/ for forbidden gemma model defaults + bad evaluator defaults.
217:
218:    Returns a list of human-readable violation strings (empty = clean). Two checks
219:    via AST (so comments/docstrings/cost-table/family-registry never false-positive):
220:
221:      1. NO live src/ os.getenv/os.environ.get may default a model env var to a
222:         ``google/gemma*`` slug. This is the structural close that stops the
223:         transparency.py / llm_provider.py class of bug from recurring.
224:      2. Any inline ``PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL`` default MUST equal the locked Mirror slug
225:         (the evaluator role maps to the Mirror per the lock's legacy_compat). A
226:         PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL with NO string default (e.g. ``os.getenv(...) or X``)
227:         passes — there is no literal default to be wrong.
228:    """
229:    violations: list[str] = []
230:    if not SRC_ROOT.exists():
231:        return violations
232:    try:
233:        mirror_slug = _mirror_slug_from_lock()
234:    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
235:        return [f"could not load lock mirror slug: {exc}"]
236:
237:    for py_path in sorted(SRC_ROOT.rglob("*.py")):
238:        rel = py_path.relative_to(REPO_ROOT).as_posix()
239:        if any(f"/{d}/" in f"/{rel}" for d in _GEMMA_GATE_EXCLUDE_DIRS):
240:            continue
241:        try:
242:            tree = ast.parse(py_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"), filename=str(py_path))
243:        except (SyntaxError, UnicodeDecodeError):
244:            continue
245:        for node in ast.walk(tree):
246:            if not isinstance(node, ast.Call):
247:                continue
248:            env_name, default_lit = _getenv_default_literal(node)
249:            if default_lit is None:
250:                continue
251:            # Check 1: forbidden gemma default for any model env var.
252:            if default_lit.lower().startswith(_GEMMA_PREFIX):
253:                violations.append(
254:                    f"{rel}:{node.lineno}: forbidden gemma default "
255:                    f"{default_lit!r} for env {env_name!r} — repoint to the locked "
256:                    f"GLM mirror (no google/gemma* runtime default; CLAUDE.md §9.1.8)"
257:                )
258:            # Check 2: inline PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL default must equal the mirror.
259:            if env_name == "PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL" and default_lit != mirror_slug:
260:                violations.append(
261:                    f"{rel}:{node.lineno}: PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL inline default "
262:                    f"{default_lit!r} != locked mirror {mirror_slug!r} — the "
263:                    f"evaluator role maps to the Mirror (lock legacy_compat)"
264:                )
265:    return violations
266:
267:
268:def gemma_gate(stream=sys.stdout) -> int:
269:    """CI conformance gate: 0 iff no forbidden gemma/evaluator default in live src/."""
270:    violations = scan_gemma_defaults()
271:    if violations:
272:        print("Gemma conformance gate: FAIL", file=stream)
273:        for v in violations:
274:            print(f"  - {v}", file=stream)
275:        return 1
276:    print(
277:        "Gemma conformance gate: OK — no google/gemma* model default and no "

1:"""Build the REAL canonical demo bundle from a real pipeline run.
2:
3:I-p2-036 (#795): the prior canonical demo bundle had PLACEHOLDER source text
4:(claim not in source = fabricated proof). This rebuilds it from a REAL run
5:(real claims + real source spans that genuinely contain them). Maps the run's
6:verified sentences (verification_details.json: sentence + token{evidence_id,
7:start,end}) + the real fetched source text (evidence_pool.json: direct_quote)
8:into the v1.0 signed-bundle format the inspector loads.
9:
10:§-1.1 gate: a sentence is featured ONLY if every numeric token in it appears in
11:the cited span (strict_verify-style numeric-fidelity re-check). Spans that fail
12:are dropped — we never ship a claim whose source span doesn't support it.
13:
14:Signature: NONE (operator chose "skip the seal" — option b). No manifest.yaml.asc
15:is written, so the inspector honestly renders signaturePresent=false.
16:"""
17:
18:from __future__ import annotations
19:
20:import hashlib
21:import json
22:import re
23:import sys
24:from pathlib import Path
25:
26:RUN = Path("outputs/honest_sweep_r3/clinical/clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm")
27:OUT = Path("web/public/canonical_bundles/v1_canonical_success")
28:DECISION_ID = "decision_real_tirzepatide_0001"
29:POOL_ID = "pool_real_tirzepatide_0001"
30:REPORT_ID = "report_real_tirzepatide_0001"
31:BUNDLE_ID = "bundle_real_tirzepatide_0001"
32:
33:_NUM = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
34:_PROV_TOKEN = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")
35:
36:
37:def strip_tokens(s: str) -> str:
38:    """Remove inline provenance tokens [#ev:id:start-end] — they are metadata,
39:    NOT claim content. (Their id/offset digits would otherwise pollute the
40:    numeric-fidelity check and the displayed claim text.)"""
41:    return _PROV_TOKEN.sub("", s).strip()
42:
43:
44:def _norm(s: str) -> str:
45:    # normalise unicode minus/hyphen variants so numeric matching is fair.
46:    return s.replace("−", "-").replace("‐", "-").replace("–", "-")
47:
48:
49:def _tier_counts(sources: list[dict]) -> dict:
50:    counts: dict[str, int] = {}
51:    for s in sources:
52:        t = s.get("tier", "T1")
53:        counts[t] = counts.get(t, 0) + 1
54:    return dict(sorted(counts.items()))
55:
56:
57:def numbers_present(sentence: str, span: str) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
58:    """Every numeric token in the sentence must appear in the span (§-1.1)."""
59:    nums = _NUM.findall(_norm(sentence))
60:    span_n = _norm(span)
61:    missing = [n for n in nums if n not in span_n]
62:    return (len(missing) == 0, missing)
63:
64:
65:def main() -> int:
66:    vd = json.loads((RUN / "verification_details.json").read_text("utf-8"))
67:    pool = json.loads((RUN / "evidence_pool.json").read_text("utf-8"))
68:    protocol = json.loads((RUN / "protocol.json").read_text("utf-8"))
69:    by_id = {s["evidence_id"]: s for s in pool}
70:
71:    sections = []
72:    cited_ids: set[str] = set()
73:    total_kept = 0
74:    total_dropped_gate = 0
75:
76:    for sec in vd["sections"]:
77:        title = sec.get("title", "Findings")
78:        vsents = []
79:        for k in sec.get("kept") or []:
80:            sentence = strip_tokens(k["sentence"])
81:            toks = k.get("tokens") or []
82:            if not toks:
83:                continue
84:            prov_tokens = []
85:            ok_any = False
86:            for tok in toks:
87:                eid = tok["evidence_id"]
88:                start, end = tok["start"], tok["end"]
89:                ev = by_id.get(eid)
90:                if not ev:
91:                    continue
92:                dq = ev.get("direct_quote", "") or ""
93:                span = dq[start:end]
94:                if not span.strip():
95:                    continue
96:                ok, missing = numbers_present(sentence, span)
97:                if not ok:
98:                    print(
99:                        f"  [§-1.1 DROP] {eid}:{start}-{end} missing nums "
100:                        f"{missing} :: {sentence[:70]!r}"
101:                    )
102:                    continue
103:                prov_tokens.append(f"[#ev:{eid}:{start}-{end}]")
104:                cited_ids.add(eid)
105:                ok_any = True
106:            if not ok_any:
107:                total_dropped_gate += 1
108:                continue
109:            vsents.append(
110:                {
111:                    "section_id": title,
112:                    "sentence_text": sentence,
113:                    "provenance_tokens": prov_tokens,
114:                    "verifier_pass": True,
115:                    "assertion_surface": "prose",
116:                    "contradiction": None,
117:                    "drop_reason": None,
118:                    "evaluator_agrees": True,
119:                    "evaluator_disagreement": None,
120:                    "is_synthesis_claim": False,
121:                }
122:            )
123:            total_kept += 1
124:        if vsents:
125:            sections.append(
126:                {
127:                    "section_id": title,
128:                    "section_title": title,
129:                    "section_status": "verified",
130:                    "section_verify_pass_rate": 1.0,
131:                    "verified_sentences": vsents,
132:                }
133:            )
134:
135:    if total_kept == 0:
136:        print("FATAL: no sentences passed the §-1.1 gate", file=sys.stderr)
137:        return 1
138:
139:    question = protocol.get("question") or protocol.get("research_question") or ""
140:

142:        "report_id": REPORT_ID,
143:        "pool_id": POOL_ID,
144:        "decision_id": DECISION_ID,

146:        "overall_verify_pass_rate": 1.0,
147:        "verifier_pass_threshold": 0.4,
148:        "pipeline_verdict": "success",
149:        "generator_model": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro",

151:        "family_segregation_passed": True,
152:        "started_at_utc": "2026-05-20T00:00:00+00:00",
153:        "finished_at_utc": "2026-05-20T00:00:00+00:00",
154:        "latency_ms": 0,
155:        "cost_usd": 0.0,
156:        "research_question": question,
157:    }
158:
159:    sources = []
160:    for eid in sorted(cited_ids):
161:        ev = by_id[eid]
162:        url = ev.get("source_url", "") or ""
163:        domain = url.split("/")[2] if "://" in url else (ev.get("source") or "")
164:        sources.append(

codex
The B10 token resolver has a split picture: the live path queries OpenRouter’s `/models` table first, but the static fallback still contains conservative verifier caps. I’m reflecting that nuance in the file so the operator can see what is paid-path-critical versus what is residual static drift.

191:
192:def _parse_models_table(data: list[dict]) -> dict[str, tuple[int, Optional[int]]]:
193:    """Reduce the raw /models ``data`` list to {slug: (context_length, completion_cap)}."""
194:    out: dict[str, tuple[int, Optional[int]]] = {}
195:    for entry in data:
196:        try:
197:            slug = entry.get("id")
198:            if not slug:
199:                continue
200:            ctx = entry.get("context_length")
201:            top = entry.get("top_provider") or {}
202:            cap = top.get("max_completion_tokens")
203:            ctx_int = int(ctx) if ctx is not None else None
204:            cap_int = int(cap) if cap is not None else None
205:            if ctx_int is None and cap_int is None:
206:                continue
207:            # If context is unknown but cap is known, treat cap as the bound;
208:            # store a large context so the cap is what binds.
209:            out[slug] = (ctx_int if ctx_int is not None else 10 ** 9, cap_int)
210:        except (TypeError, ValueError):
211:            continue
212:    return out
213:
214:
215:def _get_models_cache() -> dict[str, tuple[int, Optional[int]]]:
216:    """Return the TTL-cached parsed models table (possibly empty)."""
217:    global _models_cache, _models_cache_at
218:    ttl = _DEFAULT_MODELS_TTL
219:    try:
220:        ttl = int(os.getenv(_MODELS_TTL_ENV, str(_DEFAULT_MODELS_TTL)))
221:    except ValueError:
222:        pass
223:    now = time.monotonic()
224:    if _models_cache is not None and (now - _models_cache_at) < ttl:
225:        return _models_cache
226:    raw = _fetch_models_table()
227:    parsed = _parse_models_table(raw) if raw else {}
228:    _models_cache = parsed
229:    _models_cache_at = now
230:    return parsed
231:
232:
233:def reset_cache() -> None:
234:    """Clear the models cache (test hook)."""
235:    global _models_cache, _models_cache_at
236:    _models_cache = None
237:    _models_cache_at = 0.0

239:
306:        silently starving (Codex B10 iter-1 P1-3; LAW II / §9.1.8 never-starve).
307:
308:    Reasoning EFFORT is untouched (§9.1.8) — this only bounds completion tokens.
309:    """
310:    if not _resolver_enabled():
311:        return requested_max_tokens
312:    if requested_max_tokens is None or requested_max_tokens <= 0:
313:        return requested_max_tokens
314:
315:    limits = resolve_model_limits(model)
316:    if limits is None:
317:        # Unknown model -> PASS THROUGH (byte-identical to pre-resolver behavior).
318:        return requested_max_tokens
319:
320:    context_length, completion_cap = limits
321:    margin = _safety_margin()
322:
323:    ceiling_candidates: list[int] = []
324:    if context_length is not None:
325:        ceiling_candidates.append(int(context_length) - int(prompt_tokens) - margin)
326:    if completion_cap is not None and apply_completion_cap:
327:        ceiling_candidates.append(int(completion_cap))
328:
329:    if not ceiling_candidates:
330:        return requested_max_tokens
331:
332:    ceiling = min(ceiling_candidates)
333:
334:    if ceiling >= requested_max_tokens:
335:        # Request already fits -> no clamp. Generator full-cap path lands here.
336:        return requested_max_tokens
337:
338:    if ceiling <= 0:
339:        # The prompt alone (plus margin) overruns the window. Clamping max_tokens to
340:        # a tiny value would NOT make the request well-formed — the prompt still
341:        # exceeds context, so OpenRouter would still 400 (or, if accepted, the model
342:        # silently starves). RAISE a deterministic, LOUD prompt-too-large failure so
343:        # the genuine cause surfaces (Codex B10 iter-1 P1-3; LAW II fail-loud).
344:        msg = (
345:            f"prompt too large for model={model}: prompt~{prompt_tokens} tokens + "
346:            f"safety_margin {margin} >= context_length {context_length} "
347:            f"(completion_cap={completion_cap}); no positive completion budget fits. "
348:            f"Reduce the prompt or use a larger-context model. "
349:            f"(requested max_tokens was {requested_max_tokens}.)"
350:        )
351:        logger.error("[token-limit-resolver] FAIL-LOUD: %s", msg)
352:        raise PromptTooLargeError(msg)
353:
354:    # Genuine clamp-down: the generous request would overrun the window.
355:    logger.warning(
356:        "[token-limit-resolver] clamping max_tokens %d -> %d for model=%s "
357:        "(context_length=%s, completion_cap=%s, prompt~%d tokens, margin=%d). "
358:        "Reasoning effort unchanged; this prevents the HTTP-400 context overrun "
359:        "(the qwen-judge 400 that held the report).",
360:        requested_max_tokens, ceiling, model, context_length, completion_cap,
361:        prompt_tokens, margin,
362:    )
